CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Continued To Jan-07-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notick Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BODERO & ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion For Issuance Of Judgment.

Plaintiff's motion for issuance of judgment is granted.

The dispute here is focused on post-judgment interest. Code of Civil Procedure section 685.010 authorizes a 10 percent interest on the principal amount of a money judgment remaining unsatisfied. "A money judgment automatically accrues interest by force of law, regardless of whether it explicitly declares as much." (Hernandez v. Siegel (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 165). "Interest commences to accrue on a money judgment on the date of entry of the judgment" (CCP 685.020) and continues to accrue until the date the judgment is satisfied.

Here, this court issued a judgment based upon breach of contract for the principal amount of $53,000.00 on August 23, 2023. Defendant appealed challenging the attorney's fees award as excessive, but not challenging the principal amount. The Court of Appeal remanded the matter to reduce the attorney's fees award, leaving in place the unchallenged principal amount of the judgment. The Court of Appeal's disposition is properly treated as a modification, not a reversal, and it is appropriate to allow post-judgment interest from the date of the original judgment, i.e., August 23, 2023. (See Chodos v. Borman (2015) 239 Cal.App.4th 707, 714.) 758 days have passed since that date, and the relevant rate is 10 percent. Plaintiff is entitled to $11,006.58 in post-judgment interest. Combined with the $53,000.00 principal and $15,462.32 attorney's fees, minus Defendant's $15,050.38 in costs and fees, the net judgment amount is $64,418.52.

Therefore, the court issues judgment in favor of Plaintiff in the amount of $64,418.52.

The proposed order and proposed judgment Atty. Miyaki handed the clerk prior to open court is rejected (not verbatim) Plaintiff to resubmit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling, a proposed judgment and must drop hard courtesy copies in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs; Memorandum Of Points In Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant John Gorman's unopposed Motion to Tax Costs is GRANTED.

Neither pre-judgment nor post-judgment interest is a cost identified as allowable in Code of Civil Procedure 1033.5. It is also true that neither is a cost identified as disallowed. Generally, however, interest on a judgment is not an allowable cost under section 1033.5. Instead, pre-judgment interest must be pursued by adjudication and post-judgment interest accrues automatically on the principal amount of the judgment by operation of law. Good cause appearing, the motion to tax Plaintiff's interest cost of $10,338.24 is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
